sections.paragraph:
to cut off, to amputate, e.g. the hand, Deu 25:12 the beard, Jer 9:25 25:23 . (Arab. قَصَّ to cut the nails and hair.) See under קָצַב .

Piel קִצּץ and קִצֵּץ-

(1) to cut off, to cut asunder, e.g. a rope, Psal. 129:4 a hand, thumbs, Jud 1:6 2Sa 4:12 a spear, Psa 46:10.

(2) to divide, cut up (into threads), Exo 39:3.

(3) to cut away, to cut loose, 2Ki 18:16 24:13 .

Hophal, part. מְקֻצָּצִים, pass. of Piel No. 1, Jud 1:7. Derived noun, קֵץ (whence denom. קִיצֹון for קִצֹּון).
